skill.name: full-output-enforcement
skill.description: Overrides default LLM truncation behavior. Enforces complete code generation, bans placeholder patterns, and handles token-limit splits cleanly. Apply to any task requiring exhaustive, unabridged output.
skill.summary: Enforces complete, unabridged output from Claude and Codex by banning truncation and placeholder patterns.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged external_commands and weak_crypto patterns, but all are false positives. Line 16 contains Markdown code formatting backticks for banned patterns, not shell execution. Line 20 uses 'skeleton' to describe placeholder code, not cryptography. No executable code exists in this skill. The file is a text-based prompt instruction set with zero security risk.

content.user_title: Enforce Complete, Unabridged Output Every Time
content.value_statement: LLMs often truncate or summarize code instead of delivering complete files. This skill enforces exhaustive output by banning placeholder patterns and managing token-limit splits cleanly.
actual_capabilities: Bans placeholder comments like TODO, implement here, and similar truncated code
Prevents skeleton or summary-only responses when full implementation is requested
Handles token-limit interruptions with clean continuation markers
Enforces complete deliverable counts matching user requests
Cross-checks output against original scope before finalizing
limitations: Cannot force an LLM to generate beyond its hard token limit
Does not validate code correctness or syntax
Cannot detect when an LLM provides wrong but complete output
Depends on user specifying the expected number of deliverables

use_cases.title: Get Complete Production Code
use_cases.description: Request a full implementation of a feature, API, or module. The skill ensures no placeholder comments or skeleton code appears in the output.

use_cases.title: Generate All Items in a List
use_cases.description: Ask for 10 test cases, 5 components, or any specific number. The skill tracks count and ensures every requested item appears in the output.
use_cases.target_user: Users creating structured content or documentation

use_cases.title: Handle Long Outputs Seamlessly
use_cases.description: For lengthy codebases or documentation, the skill manages continuation cleanly with markers so no content is lost or duplicated.
use_cases.target_user: Engineers working on large-scale code generation

prompt_templates.title: Request Complete Code
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a complete [module name] with [specific requirements]. Apply the full-output-enforcement skill.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need full implementation with no placeholders

prompt_templates.title: Generate Multiple Items
prompt_templates.prompt: Create [number] of [item type]. Make sure every single one is fully written out with no shortcuts.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need a specific count of items completed

prompt_templates.title: Handle Long Output
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate the full [large feature] in its entirety. If you hit a limit, mark where you stopped and I will continue.
prompt_templates.scenario: When expecting output that may exceed token limits

prompt_templates.title: Verify Completeness
prompt_templates.prompt: Check that the output contains all [specific items] requested. Flag anything missing before finalizing.
prompt_templates.scenario: When verifying that no items were truncated or skipped

output_examples.input: Write a user authentication module with registration, login, and password reset
output_examples.output: Full implementation with all three features: registration function, login handler, password reset flow. No TODO comments. No 'implement similar pattern for X'. Complete code for each function.

output_examples.input: Create 5 API endpoint handlers for a todo app
output_examples.output: All 5 handlers fully implemented: create todo, get todos, get single todo, update todo, delete todo. Each handler includes input validation, error handling, and response formatting. No placeholder logic.

output_examples.input: Generate a complete React component library with 10 components
output_examples.output: All 10 components fully written: Button, Input, Modal, Card, Alert, Dropdown, Checkbox, Radio, Select, Textarea. Each with props interface, state management, event handlers, and styling. If interrupted at component 6, continuation marker shows [PAUSED — 6 of 10 complete. Send 'continue' to resume].

best_practices: Always specify the exact number of deliverables expected in your request
Review the banned pattern list to understand what the skill prevents
Use continuation markers when working with very large codebases
anti_patterns: Requesting 'a typical example' instead of a specific implementation
Accepting partial output when you requested a complete solution
Using vague language that allows the LLM to summarize instead of detail

faq.question: What happens if the output gets cut off due to token limits?
faq.answer: The skill marks the interruption point with a continuation marker like [PAUSED — X of Y complete. Send 'continue' to resume]. Send 'continue' and the LLM picks up exactly where it stopped.

faq.question: Can this skill make an LLM generate infinite content?
faq.answer: No. The skill does not increase token limits. It only ensures that what is generated is complete and unabridged up to the model's actual limit.

faq.question: Does this skill validate the code for errors?
faq.answer: No. The skill enforces completeness and bans placeholders. It does not check syntax, logic, or runtime correctness of the generated code.

faq.question: What are 'banned output patterns'?
faq.answer: These are placeholder phrases like '// TODO', '// implement here', '// similar to above', or 'the rest follows this pattern'. The skill treats these as hard failures.

faq.question: How does the skill handle continuation?
faq.answer: On interruption, the LLM outputs a marker with completion status and the next section name. Responding with 'continue' resumes from that exact point without recap or duplication.

faq.question: Can I use this with other skills?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill works alongside code generation, refactoring, or documentation skills. Apply it when you need those skills to produce complete rather than partial output.
